tools.name: search_memories
tools.description: Searches semantically across stored memories using Mem0. Use this to retrieve relevant context about user preferences, patterns, and past interactions before responding. This is for retrieval only - use add_memory to store new memories, and list_memories to see all memories.

query.description: Search query text to find relevant memories

limit.description: Maximum number of memories to return

user.description: User ID to search memories for (defaults to configured user)

tools.name: add_memory
tools.description: Adds new memories to Mem0 storage. Use this when user explicitly asks to remember something or to store conversation context for future retrieval. Supports two forms: simple text memory or conversation messages (auto-extracts memories via LLM). For searching stored memories, use search_memories instead.

text.description: Simple text memory to store (use instead of messages)

messages.description: Array of conversation messages to extract memories from (use instead of text)

user.description: User ID to store memory for (defaults to configured user)

tools.name: list_memories
tools.description: Lists all stored memories for a user with IDs and creation dates. Use this to see what memories are currently stored. For searching specific memories, use search_memories instead.

user.description: User ID to list memories for (defaults to configured user)

tools.name: delete_memory
tools.description: Deletes memories from Mem0 storage. Use this to remove specific memories or all memories for a user. Supports two modes: delete a specific memory by ID, or delete all memories for a user.

memory_id.description: ID of specific memory to delete (use instead of all flag)

all.description: Delete all memories for the user (use instead of memory_id)

user.description: User ID to delete memories for (required when using all flag)

messages.content: Hey, I want to check if there's any information stored about my preferences or past conversations here. Can you show me what's currently saved?

There's currently nothing stored in the memory system — the list is empty. This means there are no saved preferences or past conversation details to display.

If you'd like, I can remember something for you (just ask me to store information), or we can continue with whatever you'd like to work on next!

Great, thanks for checking. Since there's nothing stored yet, I'd like to add my first preference. Can you remember that I prefer metric units for all measurements? That would be helpful for any future calculations or data we work with.

function_call.name: skill-tools-add_memory
function_call.arguments: {"text": "User preference: metric units for all measurements (e.g., Celsius for temperature, meters/centimeters for length, liters for volume, kilograms for weight)."}

messages.content: {"memory": {"id": "m2", "user": "None", "text": "User preference: metric units for all measurements (e.g., Celsius for temperature, meters/centimeters for length, liters for volume, kilograms for weight)."}}
messages.name: skill-tools-add_memory

messages.content: Done! I've stored your preference for metric units. Now whenever we work with measurements, I'll use Celsius, meters, liters, kilograms, etc. Let me know if you'd like to add any other preferences!
Now that I've added that preference, can you show me all the memories you have stored? I want to verify that the metric units preference was actually saved correctly.

{"memories": [{"id": "m2", "user": "None", "text": "User preference: metric units for all measurements (e.g., Celsius for temperature, meters/centimeters for length, liters for volume, kilograms for weight)."}]}
messages.name: skill-tools-list_memories

messages.content:
Your metric units preference is saved correctly. There's one memory in storage:

- **ID:** m2
- **Content:** "User preference: metric units for all measurements (e.g., Celsius for temperature, meters/centimeters for length, liters for volume, kilograms for weight)."

Everything looks good! Let me know if you want to add more or make any changes.

Now that I've verified the metric units preference is stored, I'd like to clean that up. Can you remove that memory from storage? I want to make sure the system can handle removing preferences when needed.

Done! The metric units preference memory has been removed. The delete function worked correctly — the system can handle removing preferences when needed. Let me know if you'd like to start fresh or add anything else!
Thanks for confirming that worked. Can you show me the memories now to verify it's actually gone?
Confirmed — the memory list is now empty. The metric units preference has been fully removed. The storage system is working as expected!

structured_turns.user_message: Hey, I want to check if there's any information stored about my preferences or past conversations here. Can you show me what's currently saved?
structured_turns.assistant_message:
There's currently nothing stored in the memory system — the list is empty. This means there are no saved preferences or past conversation details to display.

If you'd like, I can remember something for you (just ask me to store information), or we can continue with whatever you'd like to work on next!

structured_turns.user_message: Great, thanks for checking. Since there's nothing stored yet, I'd like to add my first preference. Can you remember that I prefer metric units for all measurements? That would be helpful for any future calculations or data we work with.
structured_turns.assistant_message: Done! I've stored your preference for metric units. Now whenever we work with measurements, I'll use Celsius, meters, liters, kilograms, etc. Let me know if you'd like to add any other preferences!
